sentences.text: Hey, Cheryl.
Hey, Lindsay.
Hey, how are you?
I'm Cheryl.
I hear her around the corner.
Sounds like she's… oh, here she is.
Hello!
Sounds like she's wrapping up.
How's it been going for you guys?
There we go.
Good, how about you?
Doing well.
I've, I took the day off yesterday, so it was a long Labor Day weekend for me, but…
Thanks.
Catching up on everything.
Yeah.
You guys stay around town, or do anything fun for Labor Day?
Yeah, I was local, nothing exciting, but it was just, you know, nice to have the extra day.
Absolutely.
Did you travel and go somewhere, or…

No, I just… we actually hung out around, I'm actually just up the road from you guys in Madison, Wisconsin, so, just tried to enjoy it, like, this time of the year is the best time of the year for around here, so… we kind of did a little staycation, and just… there's also a big festival called Taste of Madison, so… Where you can go out to all the restaurants, kind of have tents around the Capitol Square, and then a bunch of different breweries.
So, yeah, it was a good time.
Cool.
Sounds good.
Sounds great.
We love Madison.
Yeah, do you guys get up here quite a bit, or when you… a couple times a year?
Yeah, a couple times a year.
Okay, where do you… where do you head to?
Well, we… generally, like, we have… we've had properties in Madison.

Right now, we don't have any in Madison proper, or Fitchburg is the closest one we have, but over the years, we've had, like, Madison proper, and, you know, we've always really liked kind of downtown, that whole area.
That's cool.
We just looked at a new deal out there that… Patrick and I, too.
I mean, we didn't get it, but we are there, like, we still want a presence out there, too, is what my plan was.
Yeah, yeah, it's a great… and, ironically enough, we, my fiance and I just moved to Fitchburg, so definitely, it's a…
What's the.
Great, great suburb area, so, yeah, definitely a nice area.
Is that kind of where your deal was at, Lindsay?
We have a property in Fitchburg, and then, we were looking in Madison.
Okay.
We didn't get it, though.
We didn't get it.
I'm super bummed.
We're still hoping… we're drafting off of the hope that there'll be another.
Okay.
Yeah, where in Fitchburg is the current property?
Noble… what would you say that would be?
Yeah, it's like, what, Fish Hatchery Road, and Oh my gosh, I'm blanking on the… like, kind of cross street that you turn.
It's called One Glen Place.
Okay.
Yeah, Fish Hatchery's, like, the main drag there in Fishburg, so that's definitely a good location.
F.
Well, awesome.
Appreciate you all jumping on then today.

I know how busy everybody's schedules are, so I really appreciate the time, and, was able to talk to Greg a few weeks ago, and I know he had shared a few, clips and kind of filled me in on, like, his processes, but, you know, would love to get to know, kind of, like you mentioned, you know, your current workflow, like, when you're looking at new deals, and you know, how you could leverage ApartmentIQ to really maximize your guys' time, but, I guess start there, and then also, if you guys had, you know, questions for me right off the bat, happy to answer that, and then, you know, can go over the platform, if that makes sense.

Yeah, I think for us, not only being able to, like, you know, spot check what we operate today, but using it for when we're, you know, so we're very involved in the process when we acquire or develop.
So, you know, we gotta vet, do our own vetting.
And, kind of bless the rents and bless the plan.
So, we'd like to see the, like.
I'm assuming you pull comp data, too, and how you pull it, what sources you're getting it from, how often you're scrubbing.
I think we kind of want to see that information, too.

we know that some services get as granular down to, like, what their pet fees are, what their admin fees are, like, those are things, like, right now, we're still old school keeping a spreadsheet and calling the cops, so it's like, we're… we're well past the point of, like, we like to think we keep up with times, and that's, like, one of the areas that we're not keeping up with, so I think it's really important for us to understand, like.
At any given moment, like, what the most recent comp study and data is.

And, if you're pulling that weekly, daily, quarterly, like, those are the things we don't remember, because I think I've done a demo with you, but it's been… maybe not you personally, but this product, but it's been at least 2 years, and I just… I just don't remember.
Yeah, no, that's totally fair, and it was kind of the same position Greg was in.

I know he was really excited about it, but hadn't seen it in a little bit, so happy to give you the overview of ApartmentIQ, you know, where we're sourcing the data, the frequency of the data, Happy to start there.
So, I guess a little bit about us.
We… we were over a 7-year-old company that started here, actually, in Madison, and what we're doing is we're automating that market survey process.
So, eliminating that process of having the manual call, so we now work with 7 of the top 10 and over 35 of the top 50.
So, some of your largest players out there, but then also even your small, under 1,000 users, 100, you know, mom and pops.
So, anybody and everybody in between really leverages ApartmentIQ differently.
Can I ask what positions are you seeing people use it?
Is it in… is it property managers, like, on site?
Is it exec level?
Is it, like, acquisitions?
Like, what do you see the most users purchasing a seat?
Yeah, so great question, and I think you'll quickly find out.
Really, there's different use cases for everybody within an organization.

So, we have over 4 different levels of permission, and basically user basis, so… They'll be, you know, your community managers, your leasing agents, they'll be able to leverage ApartmentIQ, just so they can see their property, stay up to date on their competitors, but then, obviously, there's, you know, Greg and executives like yourself that will also use this for new deals, researching, new.
markets, and then also being able to just kind of see, where trends have been going.
So we, in addition to, you know, we started… the bread and butter was our market survey.
We also now have, basically, research and development and acquisitions, use case now, too.
So we'll also have it, it's called Explore and Research.
It's, where you're able to essentially search any market, any property, even if it's a new development, just a piece of dirt.
You can essentially get a real-time market survey for that deal.
So, really to answer your question, I mean.
It…
Do you have that, or you're building that?
What did you say?
Oh, no, it's currently all live, so it's, really a multi-use, too.
I mean, like, an organization of your size easily would, you know, have over 50 users, from your on-site teams, your regional managers, your executives, and your acquisitions team as well.

So, you'll find that it's a really a one-stop shop for live, up-to-date data, whether it's on your current properties, as I mentioned, so I'll go over you'll have a My Property section where you're able to essentially keep tabs on your current portfolio and current port performance, and then you'll have that research and explore functionality, which you can control who has access to that.
Like, obviously your, you know, leasing agents aren't going to be really using the research and explore, but, more kind of for… geared towards folks like yourself to be able to see those new deals.
see how the market's been trending.
And also, we're actually getting into forecasting as well.
So, Yeah, really excited.
If it's been a couple years, it should look way different than what you've seen.
So, like I mentioned, we started as, you know, our bread and butter is definitely the market survey tool, but we've really kind of branched out now into research and development as well.
You know, Kent was the one that wanted us to check this out, so that's how long ago it was.
I was thinking 2 years, but it's been longer.
Just FYI, like, I was trying to remember, I'm like, I know I did it.
Kent asked.
Greg and I to do it.
So, if that was 4… I mean, it's been a… It's been a while, is my point, like, the year's going away.
So, can I ask, just a few more questions?

I… and I… Respectfully, though, I am asking for, like, a condensed version, like, so how would you compare to… if someone was gonna say, like, what's the difference between, like, CoStar and this product?
What… what's, like, the top thing that comes to your mind as a differentiator?
The top thing is ultimately, I mean, it's what everybody's after, is the quality of the data.
So our data is going to be updated every single day, all the way down to the unit level, and like you even mentioned, that includes fees and deposits, is something that you'll have coverage on.
And the source of the data.
So, it's the frequency, quality of the data, is essentially what it is.
So, a lot of CoStar's information is 30, 60, 90 days old, and it's really based off of… a call center, and just what's on Apartments.
com.
So our information is updated directly from the property websites themselves down to that unit level.
So you're getting much more accurate, up-to-date information.
You're not depending on a call center, which obviously involves a lot of human error and people actually picking up the phone, which doesn't happen much often anymore.
Do you pull it from the website, like, a bot pulls it from the website every day?
Yeah, so we're using scraping and AI technology, so it's 100% automated, no human input, no human error, yes.
So that is helpful.
So then I've got two more then, so how then, how do you think you compare to, like, Yardi Matrix?
Yeah, so Yardi Matrix, it's really dependent on just Yardi users.
So we… we're able to collect from, whether it's a Yardi, Appfolio, Entrata, Resman, Yeah, real page, name your… name your PMS.
We're able to… basically, we have over 150 proprietary scraping and AI bots that are able to collect that information.
And you'll see if… are you using Yardi Matrix right now?
We are, yeah.

I just… it's one of the ones that our development uses currently because we're on Yardi, and so I'm trying to understand what the differences are, but I actually don't even know that I knew that it was only Yardi, so that's even… I think I'm done with that question.
My next question is, how does it compare to HelloData?
Yeah, so it… and this will kind of piggyback with the Yardi, you'll see, also, if that's your… what your teams are using, is our interface and application is just much easier to use.
So, as I mentioned earlier, like, this is something that… you'd have over 50 users in.
It should be something that should be easy to understand and quickly get a report that you need, whether it's a leasing agent or an executive like yourself.
So… and then a Hello Data side, is that your development team using them as well?
It's just one that was… it's circulating in the industry right now.
Yeah, definitely aware of them.
So it's a newer company, similar system to what we're doing, but, you know, we're the founders and creators of this whole, concept of the AI and scraping.

So they have… it's a much smaller shop, I think they just recently got acquired as well, so… Uncertain, on, like, the actual… Longevity of the actual company itself, so they also don't have the… overall, we have… so we have over 4 years of historical data that would be at your disposal.
Because they're a newer company, I believe they only have about, like, a year and a half to maybe 2 years.
of historical data, so I know a lot of folks just off of having that historical information.
And then they don't have the… the explore functionality that I mentioned.
So, A lot of their data is collected from Apartments.
com, which… is basically then just regurgitating the CoStar information that we kind of already hit on.
So, again, their information is a lot more capped.
Like, if you go to Apartments.
com and look at the floor plans.
you're only gonna see 4 or 5 units by floor plan on Apartments.
com, that's just the way it goes.
So when we go to the actual website, we're exposing much more information for you.
I thought… I thought I was in the impression they do website and ILS.
Do you guys do both website and ILS, or do you only bank on website?
So, in a rare case that we can't get any information, we will go to an ILS, but, that's really a huge differentiator with us.
So, to their point, I think even on their website, they say they only collect maybe, like, around 200,000 websites, we collect well over a million.
So you're… just the quality of the data from the direct websites.
is gonna be much more accurate.
So, because obviously then if your unit counts and available units are off, then that just spot… all your performance metrics are then going to be off.
So you're saying the ILSs are typically more stale than the website, is your point?
Exactly.
They're more stale, and then they also… if you go to an Apartments.
com listing, they also… all their availability is usually capped at, like, around 5 to 8, we'll say for sure under 10 units by floor plan.
You'll see with ApartmentIQ, because we're going to that leasing portal section.
of, the website.
We're actually able to expose, you know.
50, 60, 70 units that are available.
You just get much more availability into the actual units that are available.
This is so interesting, because we use Yardi websites, too, so we push our live availability, our specials, all through an ILS syndication that's built in in Yardi.
So, like, ours is the same time.
The push is the same time, to our website, to our ILS.
So, when you're saying that the ILSs are more stale, that's why I was like, they are.
I mean, I could understand a day or two, but I didn't realize it was significant as you're… Saying it is.
Yeah, and that's… I mean, so… you guys, obviously, like, your pricing and availability and specials is gonna be accurate.
I'm not saying that that's not accurate, but then a lot of times, not everybody has that Yardi.
There's a lot of folks that are on Intrada, RealPage, Resman, so that's a whole other section of the actual data that's out there.
And then, what I'm getting at is the actual units that will then show available on… on Apartments.
com versus the amount of units that you can see available on your website.
Huh.
just the way that ILSs are set up, a lot of times they'll cap just, hey, they're 2 bedrooms, they have, you know, 5 to 10 of them.
There are 3 bedrooms, they have 5 to 10 of them.
When, if you actually went to the website, you would see that, hey, they have 23 2-beds available.
Does that… does that make sense?
Yeah.
Okay.
Thank you.
Yeah, I guess… so those are really kind of the big differentiators.

I would ultimately say you'll see, versus a Yardi Matrix and a HelloData, our platform is just much more in-depth, easier to use, less clunky is generally the feedback, and we just have been doing it longer, with more data.
Are you guys still an independent company?
Yeah, yeah.
Yeah, you've not been snapped up by one of the giants yet.
No, and actually, our… close with the CEO, have no intention of doing so, so we've been around, like I mentioned, for over 7 years, profitable company, independent company, Yeah, with no intention.
You'll see… so we're over a 100-person company, just all here at ApartmentIQ.
We have over 40 engineers, so it's really cool to see the product grow.
I mean, you'll see product updates, our partners get product updates at least Every other week, if not more.

So, Big advantage, too, is that you have that direct line of communication, like, product feedback is critical for us, and you'll see that all the bells and whistles are direct feedback from our current partners.
Yeah, when you said, Hello, Datto was acquired, that was the first thing, I'm like, you're, you're probably next.
I'm surprised you guys haven't been yet.
Like, RealPage is coming for you!
That's always my question, I'm like, are you gonna sell to RealPage?
Because, you know…
That's, that's RealPage's model.
They don't build things, they just buy things, but…
Who did, who did Hello Data get acquired by, do you know?
Yeah, Grace Hill.
Oh, yeah, yeah, we saw that.
Actually, I've seen that, like, out in the postings and stuff, yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, so I don't know, it kind of took… I think general feedback was it was, like, an interesting acquire, just because I don't… it seems like not a lot of, overlap there, it seems…
happening.
like, not the same… what… I guess it is what it is.
Happy for them.
But… but yeah, that's, that's really the rundown.
I mean, like, even myself, I've been here for over 6 years now, so, really know the product inside and out, and happy to kind of show… show you guys what it's all about.
Anything else?
I appreciate the questions, you came ready, though.
Those are… those are great.
Let me just pop in the… platform here.
Can you see this?
Yes.
Yep.

We'll zoom in to… it looks like you guys got a good TV display, but, so the three sections I mentioned, you'll have the My Properties, which all this would be the actual existing portfolio would all be under My Properties.
Then you'd have the Research and Explore.
And that's gonna be that section, like I mentioned, that you'll be able to see any sort of nationwide access, any sort of market, sub-market you can search by.
You can get very specific, but I'll briefly just touch in here to see for your current portfolio, what this would look like.
You'll have your subject property, And then versus your competitors.
So, your general question is, like, how do we select the competitors?
Who selects those?
Just right off the bat, anybody with a website, you can track, but we'll do a standard comp match algorithm, but as you can see in here, you can add and remove your properties as you wish.
So, you guys know the market the best, but we'll suggest A 1 to 100 score, 100 would be the highest.
Hey, these are the closest competitors that you should be looking at, but as you can see in here, you can simply click X to remove.
Or simply hit Add Comp and type in the address or property name and add it to your comp set.
So, looking at your…
How that score… is that comp score?
Is that, like, age, quality, rent rate?
Like, what's the… what drives that?
Yeah, so it's… it's an algorithm, and it's just taking in the factors, the distance, the year built, the rent rates, and the amenities.
So just, based off of that, it will tell you your closest competitors.
But yeah, right in here, too, you can filter, so maybe you just wanted to look at specific amenities.
specific property class, units, you're built, you can filter by all of that, to whatever's most important to you.
Again, it's just a suggestion.
It's another big advantage that you have over, like, a CoStar.
We've heard that it's difficult to swap in and out comps.
Same with Yardi Matrix.
Here you have that ability to do it as you wish within seconds.
What we're collecting, though, as you'll see, we're giving you Google reviews, we're giving you the average rent rates, we're giving you the concessions as well.
So, concessions coverage is something that is second to none here.
Because of our AI technology, we're able to pick up on whether it's a pop-up, a banner, a baked-in image.
It's also very, it's a very smart system, if you will, and you can see it even is able to differentiate between different concessions.
So if it's a two-month free on studios versus a two and a half months free on ones, twos, and threes, it's able to apply when it's different.
Okay.
Into the availability.
So I mentioned just being able to see… more availability is always what everybody's after.
So you're able to select… we have over 30 different customized metrics, but I'll just, like, pop on, like, vacant and available units in here for you to see.
You're able to see, I mean, there's properties that we're, you know, collecting 79 available units, 46 plus units, so much more than you would be able to see just at a glance on Apartments.
com.
you're able to see even applications, cancels, denials, and then we even calculate and advertise occupancy.
So, like, Helodata, for an example, they only will give you a leased percentage.
Our system's much more advanced and able to pick up on what's available and what's vacant.
You're able to see, then, the leases.
In any one of these metrics, you can filter high to low, or low to high by simply clicking on it.
So right away, you can see 1111 Church has had the most leases with 11 last 7 days.

If you wanted to see what those 11 leases are, you can click on the actual metric and see all the way down to unit level, floor plan, concession, concession percentage, rent rate, all just right at your fingertips.
So you can see exactly what is leasing.
Cool.
As you go down, you'll get that pricing availability broken out by floor plan.
You also get those fees and deposits.
So fees and deposits are right in here.
And then both in-unit and community-level amenities.
So we're able to pick up on all the amenities, and then even the unit-specific amenities, which is very, obviously useful.
And I'll get into the more that's also used for pricing.
Are you guys… remind me again, are you manually setting your rent rates, or do you use an LRO, or…
We're on, Arum right now.
Okay.

I'd have to say at least, like, 75%, if not more, of our partners use us in conjunction, so I'll be able to show you then, too, where a lot of folks like to have us popped up when they're on those Arum pricing calls.
Really kind of gives a blueprint to the black box that sometimes Arum can be.
Any questions on the market survey portion?
I can jump ahead, Anything that you're expecting to see here?
How does it know occupancy versus leased?
Yeah, great question.
So we're… any one of the metrics, too, has an actual pop-up box for the definition.
So lease percentage is just gonna… it's gonna include the upcoming move-in dates.

So, what we were able to pick up on… If it's not… if it's out in the future, it's gonna be actually a vacant… so if it's gone past, like, it was available today, and then it's also available tomorrow.
based on that move-in date, we know it's vacant.
And then lease will kind of just give you… that's why the lease percentage is generally a little bit higher.
Does that make sense?
Yep.
And then that's also then how we're differentiating between available units and vacant units.
So, vacant units is available, leased, or applied within the next 10 days.
Again, if you wanted to look into any one of those units, you can click on the pop-up box and get that full breakdown.
So essentially, too, the really cool thing is we have full rent rolls of any property.
You can Pop into the property, and you can see leased units in all units.
So this will give you a breakdown of the unit mix of the property, available versus lease price per square foot, available versus lease rent, and net effective rent.
How do you get the rat rolls for all the property?

Just because we've been… so it's all… it's all assumed, but because we've been tracking these properties, you know, for 5 plus years, we essentially will show you the last time the unit was leased and for what price.
Oh, okay.
And then we also know what's available, and what's been applied for.
So, let me ask you this, for this is, you know, gonna be applicable to a lot of your clients, then, if they're using, you know, pricing software, too.
how does it know, because there's, like, a range, right?
Where it's like, if I take a 15-month lease, my rate might be lower than if I take a 10-month lease.
How does it know, or is it just assuming that somebody picked the lowest or the best… Available rate.
Yeah, good question.
So, we're always going to assume that the best available rate was selected.
So, obviously they could have done a shorter term for the higher price, but we want to make sure that you always have the best available price, at your fingertips here.
Got it.

Next, I think this is the… our most highly used, and I really like this report, just to be able to… this incorporates that 4 plus years of historical data that we have, but then also compares it with current performance.
So right here, I can select whatever timeframe I want to look at.
A lot of folks like to jump back and forth between, like, the 30, 90 days, so I can look over the last 30 days, over the last month, how my property's been performing by floor plan.
So you can see, price to square foot, I'll filter that high to low.
You can see for studios, you're kind of right in the middle, and you've had to decrease by about 6% here in the last 30 days.
You can also see you've leased 2 studios, and the leader is 805 Lee here, and they've leased 8 in the last 30 days.
And this just, as you scroll down, just goes 1s, twos, and threes.
This, too, you can filter, like, if you just wanted to look at specific properties.
like, I just want to look at, you know, these three specific properties, you can really condense it down and make it easy to understand.
Also, if you wanted to look at specific Size thresholds, specific bathroom bed types, you can do that in here as well.
All of our reporting, too, is exportable and automated.
So, you get both a PDF and an Excel option.
You simply would put in the email addresses that you want sent to, and then what cadence.
So, a lot of folks like it, you know, delivered on Monday or Tuesday morning.
They would get this report, and it'd be sent right into your inbox.
So I'll show you what that looks like as well.
Is all yours right now, like, in Excel, it sounded like?
For all of our what in Excel?
all of, like, your current market surveys, is that, like, filled out via Excel?
Essentially, yeah.
Okay, so you can get ex-.
We don't really do it in that way anymore.
It's like our pricing guy keeps track of some things, but our site teams aren't doing, this anymore.
We're really reliant on ARM.
Okay.
So yeah, if you're relying on air, this will give you visibility into exactly who those comps are, rather than just high-level, you know, averages.
Yep.
so, here's, like, the PDF, it will show you broken out by studios, ones, twos, and threes.
Then we'll even have a concessions report at the end, so you can see, hey, 3 of my 3 comps are offering a concession.
Here are the concession details.
And then even, fees and deposits.
breakdown.
I know you're mentioning how much, more important this is, I know, for a lot of folks.
Yeah.
So you'll have all this, automated.
As you can see, too, you can come on in here and just export it however you wish.
Lastly, then, we have the historicals report.
So this is where you have… it's actually going to be, cra… it will be 5 years coverage here next month, so it goes back to October of 2020.

You'll have the timeframe that you can select from, so whatever time frame, so this is showing you last 12 months, and you can look at it, you know, weekly over week, month over month, quarter over quarter.
This will show you your current property, which is the purple line, versus the market itself.
So I'll clear back to all the properties in your comp set, and you can see how your pricing has been trending versus your competitors.
Concessions, so you can see what time of the year concession's offering the most, or the least.
who's the concession leader.
The one I really like to show and highlight is you're able to see leased units by property by month.
So you're able to see exactly how many leases you're capturing versus your competitors, you know, when is the slow leasing season.
there's so many use cases in here.
I know, like, even marketing folks, like, hey, we deployed a new marketing concession here in February, and then we collected 21 and 13 leases the next couple months.
So it's really easy to kind of… See exactly how you've been competing here.
I like that one a lot.
And then there's also leased and exposure percentage that you can toggle between below here.
If you know, like, if you're tracking the units that are being, recaptured over the four years, do you have any, like, renewal statistics, or no?
Not renewal statistics right now.
What people are doing, I do know, is there… any one of these, like, the rent rolls that we're seeing, you can go ahead in here and export this.
So do you… remember Greg was mentioning, are you guys using, like, any Power BI tools?
- or not.
Okay, so you can just export this and feed this into Excel, and then you can make your charts from that information.
We don't have it calculated, though, in-app.
Just, just curious, yeah.
Seems like you have all the right information for it, so I was just trying to…
Yeah, we definitely do, and we're actually pivoting that.
That will be kind of in our forecasting, so kind of a… any questions on the… that was a good segue into Explore, if you have any questions on this portion, though.
No, I don't think so.
Thank you.
Great, so that will be the… for all your existing properties.
Then we have the Explore, so this is where you can search any market, sub-market.
And be able to instantly see rent performance, year-over-year growth.
New supply and demographic information.
So, This can be, again, any market, MSA, neighborhood, zip code, and you'll see on the right-hand side, you can create custom boundaries.

So if you just wanted to look at, like, this couple mile radius, maybe 5 miles in North Dallas, you can go ahead and click Apply, and it will create a new custom boundary and updated metrics for you in here.
Wow, neat.
You also then will get a property list, so you can see all.
Yay.
He's within this area.
You'll be able to see ownership information, transactional data.
Wow.
that… Can you hear me?
I know there was, like, a loud plane that just flew over.
Oh, yeah, no, we gotcha.
Okay.
So I saw something on the screen prior that caught my eye as you were flipping to this.
It talked about, like, the number of units under construction.
Does it have, like, plans, all that?
Yes, good call.
It's a new supply we have under construction and scheduled.
Scheduled, got it, okay.
